zh.lifeSummary: 當Rasse III Van Gavere Lord of Chieves Count of Everghem Baron of Flanders於1112年出生在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫加維時，他的父親Rasso De Gavere24歲，母親Elizabeth Boudewijn van Gent22歲。他和Eva Ida Chievres Dormison de Peteghem至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1150年在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德盧庫特享年38歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rasse III Van Gavere Lord of Chieves Count of Everghem Baron of Flanders于1112年出生在比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫加維时，他的父亲Rasso De Gavere24岁，母亲Elizabeth Boudewijn van Gent22岁。他和Eva Ida Chievres Dormison de Peteghem至少有2个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1150年在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德盧庫特享年38岁。

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Chieves
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Scottish   Cheves  .

variants.name: Cheves
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire): possibly a variant of English   Chevis  . Compare   Cheeves  .
Spanish: variant of   Chevez  .
History: Langdon Cheves (1776–1857), a congressman and financier, was born in Abbeville District, SC. His father was Alexander Chivas, of Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

variants.name: Gavitt
meanings: Altered form of English (Jersey and Guernsey) Gavey: probably from the Middle English (Old French) female personal name Geve, a pet form of French Genevieve, from ancient Germanic Genovefa (see Cheeves ).

nameMeanings.name: Count
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earl
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Flanders
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name from Middle English Fla(u)ndres ‘Flanders’, denoting someone from Flanders, a historical region covering parts of modern-day Belgium, France, and the Netherlands. Compare Fleming .

variants.name: Fleming
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, from Middle English fleming. The word reflects a Norman French form of Old French flamanc ‘Fleming’, from the stem flam- + the ancient Germanic suffix -ing. In the Middle Ages there was considerable commercial intercourse between England and the Netherlands, particularly in the wool trade, and many Flemish weavers and dyers settled in England. This surname is also common in south and east Scotland and in Ireland, where it is sometimes found in the Gaelicized form Pléamonn.
German: variant of   Flemming  , cognate with 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Rasse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German: unexplained.
English: variant of   Pass  .
French: nickname from Old French passe ‘sparrow’. This surname is also found in Haiti.

variants.name: Pass
meanings: English: from a pet form of the medieval personal name Passe, probably a pet form of Pascal .
German: topographic name from Pass ‘pass, passage’ (from Middle Low German pas ‘pace, passage way, water gauge’).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name or nickname from Yiddish and Polish pas ‘belt, girdle’.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: Van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Ivan   or   Evan  , as in the case of the American film actor  Van Heflin  (  1910–71 , born  Emmett Evan Heflin ) and the Northern Irish singer  Van Morrison  (  b. 1945  as  George Ivan Morrison ).

nameMeanings.name: Gavere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois, Michel.
Altered form of French   Lariviere  .

variants.name: Lariviere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Normand, Fernand, Marcel, Adelard, Andre, Gaston, Philibert, Aime, Alcide, Alphonse, Antoine.
French (Larivière):
habitational name from any of numerous places in various parts of France named Larivière or La Rivière, from Old French riviere ‘river, shore’.

en.lifeSummary: When Rasse III Van Gavere Lord of Chieves Count of Everghem Baron of Flanders was born in 1112, in Gavere, East Flanders, Belgium, his father, Rasso De Gavere, was 24 and his mother, Elizabeth Boudewijn van Gent, was 22. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Eva Ida Chievres Dormison de Peteghem. He died in 1150, in Roucourt, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, at the age of 38.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rasse III Van Gavere Lord of Chieves Count of Everghem Baron of Flanders est né en 1112, à Gavere, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, son père, Rasso De Gavere, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Boudewijn van Gent, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Eva Ida Chievres Dormison de Peteghem. Il est décédé en 1150, à Roucourt, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, à l’âge de 38 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rasse III Van Gavere Lord of Chieves Count of Everghem Baron of Flanders 1112 in Gavere, Ostflandern, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rasso De Gavere 24 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Boudewijn van Gent 22. Er hatte mit Eva Ida Chievres Dormison de Peteghem mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1150 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Roucourt, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rasse III Van Gavere Lord of Chieves Count of Everghem Baron of Flanders è nato il nel 1112 nel Gavere, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio, il suo padre, Rasso De Gavere, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Boudewijn van Gent, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Eva Ida Chievres Dormison de Peteghem. Morì nel 1150, nel Roucourt, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, all'età di 38 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Rasse III Van Gavere Lord of Chieves Count of Everghem Baron of Flandersが1112年にベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンガバーで生まれた時，父Rasso De Gavereは24歳，母Elizabeth Boudewijn van Gentは22歳であった。彼はEva Ida Chievres Dormison de Peteghemとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1150年にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードロウコウルトで38歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Rasse III Van Gavere Lord of Chieves Count of Everghem Baron of Flanders(이)가 1112년 벨기에 스트-블라아더 게이버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rasso De Gavere(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Boudewijn van Gent(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Eva Ida Chievres Dormison de Peteghem(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1150년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 루쿠르트에서 38세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rasse III Van Gavere Lord of Chieves Count of Everghem Baron of Flanders nasceu em 1112, em Gavere, Flandres Oriental, Bélgica, seu pai, Rasso De Gavere, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Boudewijn van Gent, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Eva Ida Chievres Dormison de Peteghem. Ele faleceu em 1150, em Roucourt, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, com 38 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rasse III Van Gavere Lord of Chieves Count of Everghem Baron of Flanders родился в 1112 г. в местности гавир, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Rasso De Gavere было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Boudewijn van Gent, 22. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Eva Ida Chievres Dormison de Peteghem. Он умер в 1150 г. в местности роукоурт, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция в возрасте 38 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rasse III Van Gavere Lord of Chieves Count of Everghem Baron of Flanders nació en 1112, en Gavere, Flandes Oriental, Bélgica, su papá, Rasso De Gavere, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Boudewijn van Gent, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Eva Ida Chievres Dormison de Peteghem. Murió en 1150, en Roucourt, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, a la edad de 38 años.
